title.raw: 《司馬相如列傳》 Biography of Sima Xiangru

sentences.zh: 司馬相如者，蜀郡成都人也，字長卿。

translations.literal: Sima Xiangru was a man of Chengdu in Shu Commandery, styled Changqing.
translations.idiomatic: Sima Xiangru was a native of Chengdu in Shu Commandery, courtesy name Changqing.

sentences.zh: 少時好讀書，學擊劍，故其親名之曰犬子。

translations.literal: In his youth he loved reading and studied swordsmanship, and so his parents named him Quanzi (Puppy).
translations.idiomatic: As a boy he loved books and studied swordsmanship, and his parents gave him the childhood name Quanzi, meaning 'puppy.'

sentences.zh: 相如既學，慕藺相如之為人，更名相如。

translations.literal: After Xiangru completed his studies, he admired the character of Lin Xiangru, and changed his name to Xiangru.
translations.idiomatic: When he had completed his studies, he came to admire the character of the ancient statesman Lin Xiangru and changed his own name to Xiangru in homage.

sentences.zh: 以貲為郎，事孝景帝，為武騎常侍，非其好也。

translations.literal: By wealth qualification he became a gentleman, served Emperor Jing, and was appointed Mounted Guard Attendant, which was not to his liking.
translations.idiomatic: He purchased a position as a court gentleman and served Emperor Jing as a Mounted Guard Attendant, though the post held no appeal for him.

sentences.zh: 會景帝不好辭賦，是時梁孝王來朝，從游說之士齊人鄒陽、淮陰枚乘、吳莊忌夫子之徒，相如見而說之，因病免，客游梁。

translations.literal: As Emperor Jing did not appreciate rhapsody, at that time King Xiao of Liang came to court, accompanied by persuaders and scholars such as Zou Yang of Qi, Mei Cheng of Huaiyin, and Master Zhuang Ji of Wu and their circle; Xiangru saw them and was pleased, and so pleading illness he resigned and traveled to Liang as a guest.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Jing had no taste for literary rhapsodies. It happened that King Xiao of Liang came to court, attended by a retinue of rhetoricians and scholars — Zou Yang of Qi, Mei Cheng of Huaiyin, Master Zhuang Ji of Wu, and others of their circle. Xiangru was delighted by their company and, pleading illness, resigned his post to travel to Liang as a guest.

sentences.zh: 梁孝王令與諸生同舍，相如得與諸生游士居數歲，乃著子虛之賦。

translations.literal: King Xiao of Liang ordered him to lodge with the other scholars, and Xiangru lived among the scholars and traveling gentlemen for several years, then composed the Rhapsody of Sir Fantasy.
translations.idiomatic: King Xiao of Liang lodged him alongside the other scholars, and Xiangru spent several years in the company of these learned men and wandering intellectuals before composing his 'Rhapsody of Sir Fantasy.'

sentences.zh: 會梁孝王卒，相如歸，而家貧，無以自業。

translations.literal: As King Xiao of Liang died, Xiangru returned home, but his family was poor and he had no means of self-support.
translations.idiomatic: When King Xiao of Liang died, Xiangru returned home, only to find himself in poverty with no means of making a living.

sentences.zh: 素與臨邛令王吉相善，吉曰：「長卿久宦遊不遂，而來過我。」

translations.literal: He had long been on good terms with Wang Ji, the magistrate of Linqiong, and Ji said: 'Changqing, since your long pursuit of an official career has not succeeded, come stay with me.'
translations.idiomatic: He had long been friends with Wang Ji, the magistrate of Linqiong. Wang Ji told him, 'Changqing, your years seeking a career at court have come to nothing — come and stay with me.'

sentences.zh: 於是相如往，舍都亭。

translations.literal: Thereupon Xiangru went and lodged at the capital district pavilion.
translations.idiomatic: So Xiangru traveled there and took up lodgings at the district guesthouse.

sentences.zh: 臨邛令繆為恭敬，日往朝相如。

translations.literal: The magistrate of Linqiong feigned great deference and went daily to pay respects to Xiangru.
translations.idiomatic: The magistrate of Linqiong made an elaborate show of deference, visiting Xiangru every day to pay his respects.

sentences.zh: 相如初尚見之，后稱病，使從者謝吉，吉愈益謹肅。

translations.literal: Xiangru at first still received him, but later he pleaded illness and sent an attendant to decline for him; Ji became even more respectful and solemn.
translations.idiomatic: At first Xiangru still granted him audiences, but later he pleaded illness and sent a servant to make his excuses. Wang Ji only grew more deferential.

sentences.zh: 臨邛中多富人，而卓王孫家僮八百人，程鄭亦數百人，二人乃相謂曰：「令有貴客，為具召之。」

translations.literal: In Linqiong there were many wealthy families; Zhuo Wangsun had eight hundred household servants, and Cheng Zheng also had several hundred. The two men said to each other: 'The magistrate has a distinguished guest; let us prepare a feast and invite him.'
translations.idiomatic: Linqiong boasted many wealthy families. Zhuo Wangsun kept a household of eight hundred servants, and Cheng Zheng had several hundred of his own. These two men said to one another, 'The magistrate has a distinguished guest — let us arrange a banquet and invite him.'

sentences.zh: 并召令。

translations.literal: They also invited the magistrate.
translations.idiomatic: They invited the magistrate as well.

sentences.zh: 令既至，卓氏客以百數。

translations.literal: When the magistrate arrived, the Zhuo household's guests numbered in the hundreds.
translations.idiomatic: By the time the magistrate arrived, the Zhuo household had assembled guests numbering in the hundreds.

sentences.zh: 至日中，謁司馬長卿，長卿謝病不能往，臨邛令不敢嘗食，自往迎相如。

translations.literal: By midday, they sent an invitation to Sima Changqing, but Changqing declined pleading illness and could not go. The magistrate of Linqiong did not dare to begin eating, and personally went to welcome Xiangru.
translations.idiomatic: At noon they sent word to Sima Changqing, but he declined, pleading illness. The magistrate of Linqiong did not dare touch the food and went in person to fetch Xiangru.

sentences.zh: 相如不得已，彊往，一坐盡傾。

translations.literal: Xiangru had no choice but to reluctantly go, and the entire company was captivated by him.
translations.idiomatic: Xiangru had no choice but to go, and the moment he arrived, every guest was captivated.

sentences.zh: 酒酣，臨邛令前奏琴曰：「竊聞長卿好之，願以自娛。」

translations.literal: When the wine was flowing freely, the magistrate of Linqiong came forward with a zither and said: 'I have heard that Changqing is fond of this; may it serve as his entertainment.'
translations.idiomatic: When the wine had loosened everyone's spirits, the magistrate of Linqiong brought forward a zither and said, 'I hear that Changqing is fond of the instrument — perhaps he might play for his own amusement.'

sentences.zh: 相如辭謝，為鼓一再行。

translations.literal: Xiangru politely declined, then played one or two pieces.
translations.idiomatic: Xiangru made a show of modest refusal, then played a piece or two.

sentences.zh: 是時卓王孫有女文君新寡，好音，故相如繆與令相重，而以琴心挑之。

translations.literal: At that time Zhuo Wangsun had a daughter, Wenjun, who was newly widowed and loved music; thus Xiangru had colluded with the magistrate to build up his prestige, and used the zither's sentiments to court her.
translations.idiomatic: Now Zhuo Wangsun had a daughter named Wenjun who had recently been widowed and was fond of music. This was precisely why Xiangru had conspired with the magistrate to build up his prestige — he intended to court her through the language of the zither.

sentences.zh: 相如之臨邛，從車騎，雍容閒雅甚都；

translations.literal: When Xiangru came to Linqiong, he arrived with carriages and horsemen, his bearing elegant, leisurely, and very handsome;
translations.idiomatic: When Xiangru had first arrived in Linqiong, he came with a retinue of carriages and riders, carrying himself with a grace and elegance that was exceedingly striking;

sentences.zh: 及飲卓氏，弄琴，文君竊從戶窺之，心悅而好之，恐不得當也。

translations.literal: When he drank at the Zhuo household and played the zither, Wenjun secretly peeped at him through the door, her heart delighted and attracted, yet she feared she might not be worthy of him.
translations.idiomatic: and when he drank at the Zhuo household and played the zither, Wenjun peeked at him through the doorway. Her heart stirred with delight and longing, though she feared she might not be worthy of such a man.

sentences.zh: 既罷，相如乃使人重賜文君侍者通殷勤。

translations.literal: When the feast was over, Xiangru sent someone with generous gifts to Wenjun's attendants to convey his deep affections.
translations.idiomatic: After the banquet, Xiangru sent lavish gifts to Wenjun's attendants, conveying through them his heartfelt intentions.

sentences.zh: 文君夜亡奔相如，相如乃與馳歸成都。

translations.literal: Wenjun eloped with Xiangru by night, and Xiangru then rode swiftly back to Chengdu with her.
translations.idiomatic: That night, Wenjun fled to Xiangru, and the two of them rode swiftly back to Chengdu together.

sentences.zh: 家居徒四壁立。

translations.literal: At home there were only four bare walls standing.
translations.idiomatic: At home they found nothing but four bare walls.

sentences.zh: 卓王孫大怒曰：「女至不材，我不忍殺，不分一錢也。」

translations.literal: Zhuo Wangsun was furious and said: 'My daughter is utterly worthless. I cannot bring myself to kill her, but I will not give her a single coin.'
translations.idiomatic: Zhuo Wangsun was furious. 'That girl is utterly worthless,' he declared. 'I cannot bring myself to kill her, but she will not get a single coin from me.'

sentences.zh: 人或謂王孫，王孫終不聽。

translations.literal: Some people tried to persuade Wangsun, but Wangsun would not listen to the end.
translations.idiomatic: Some tried to talk Wangsun around, but he refused to listen.

sentences.zh: 文君久之不樂，曰：「長卿第俱如臨邛，從昆弟假貸猶足為生，何至自苦如此！」

translations.literal: Wenjun was unhappy for a long time and said: 'Changqing, let us go together to Linqiong; borrowing from my brothers and kinsmen would be enough to live on — why should we torment ourselves like this!'
translations.idiomatic: After a long spell of unhappiness, Wenjun said, 'Changqing, let us go to Linqiong together. We can borrow from my brothers and kinsmen — it would be enough to live on. Why torment ourselves like this?'

sentences.zh: 相如與俱之臨邛，盡賣其車騎，買一酒舍酤酒，而令文君當鑪。

translations.literal: Xiangru went with her to Linqiong, sold all his carriages and horses, bought a wine shop to sell wine, and had Wenjun tend the counter.
translations.idiomatic: Xiangru went with her to Linqiong, sold all their carriages and horses, and bought a small wine shop. He had Wenjun serve behind the counter.

sentences.zh: 相如身自著犢鼻褌，與保庸雜作，滌器於市中。

translations.literal: Xiangru himself wore a calf-nose loincloth, and worked alongside hired laborers, washing dishes in the marketplace.
translations.idiomatic: Xiangru himself wore a coarse loincloth and worked alongside the hired hands, washing dishes in the middle of the marketplace.

sentences.zh: 卓王孫聞而恥之，為杜門不出。

translations.literal: Zhuo Wangsun heard of this and was ashamed, and shut his gate and did not go out.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhuo Wangsun heard about this, he was so mortified that he shut his gates and refused to go out.

sentences.zh: 昆弟諸公更謂王孫曰：「有一男兩女，所不足者非財也。

translations.literal: His brothers and various gentlemen in turn told Wangsun: 'You have one son and two daughters; what you lack is not wealth.
translations.idiomatic: His brothers and other prominent men urged Wangsun, 'You have one son and two daughters — what you lack is certainly not wealth.

sentences.zh: 今文君已失身於司馬長卿，長卿故倦游，雖貧，其人材足依也，且又令客，獨柰何相辱如此！」

translations.literal: Now Wenjun has already given herself to Sima Changqing; Changqing is weary of his wanderings, and though he is poor, his talent is worth relying on. Moreover, he is a guest of the magistrate — how can you disgrace them both like this!'
translations.idiomatic: Wenjun has already given herself to Sima Changqing. He may be weary from his travels and poor, but his talent is something one can depend on. And he is a guest of the magistrate, no less — how can you humiliate them like this?'

sentences.zh: 卓王孫不得已，分予文君僮百人，錢百萬，及其嫁時衣被財物。

translations.literal: Zhuo Wangsun had no choice and gave Wenjun a hundred servants, a million coins, and the clothing and wedding goods from her original marriage.
translations.idiomatic: Zhuo Wangsun had no choice but to relent. He gave Wenjun a hundred servants, a million coins, and the clothing and possessions from her original dowry.

sentences.zh: 文君乃與相如歸成都，買田宅，為富人。

translations.literal: Wenjun then returned to Chengdu with Xiangru, bought fields and a residence, and became wealthy.
translations.idiomatic: Wenjun and Xiangru returned to Chengdu, purchased farmland and a house, and lived as people of means.

sentences.zh: 居久之，蜀人楊得意為狗監，侍上。

translations.literal: After a long time, the man of Shu named Yang Deyi served as Superintendent of Hunting Dogs and attended the emperor.
translations.idiomatic: A long while later, a fellow from Shu named Yang Deyi, who served as Superintendent of the Imperial Hounds, was attending the emperor.

sentences.zh: 上讀子虛賦而善之，曰：「朕獨不得與此人同時哉！」

translations.literal: The emperor read the Rhapsody of Sir Fantasy and praised it, saying: 'How unfortunate that I alone cannot live at the same time as this person!'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor read the 'Rhapsody of Sir Fantasy' and admired it greatly. 'What a pity,' he sighed, 'that I was not born in the same age as this man!'

sentences.zh: 得意曰：「臣邑人司馬相如自言為此賦。」

translations.literal: Deyi said: 'Your subject's fellow townsman Sima Xiangru says he composed this rhapsody.'
translations.idiomatic: Yang Deyi spoke up: 'Your servant's townsman Sima Xiangru claims to have written this very rhapsody.'

sentences.zh: 上驚，乃召問相如。

translations.literal: The emperor was surprised and thereupon summoned and questioned Xiangru.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor was astonished and immediately summoned Xiangru for an audience.

sentences.zh: 相如曰：「有是。

translations.literal: Xiangru said: 'It is so.
translations.idiomatic: Xiangru confirmed, 'Indeed, I wrote it.

sentences.zh: 然此乃諸侯之事，未足觀也。

translations.literal: However, this only describes the affairs of the feudal lords and is not worth looking at.
translations.idiomatic: But it merely describes the affairs of feudal lords and is hardly worth Your Majesty's attention.

sentences.zh: 請為天子游獵賦，賦成奏之。」

translations.literal: Please allow me to compose a rhapsody on the Son of Heaven's imperial hunt; when it is completed I shall present it.'
translations.idiomatic: Allow me to compose a rhapsody on the Son of Heaven's imperial hunt. When it is finished, I shall present it to you.'

sentences.zh: 上許，令尚書給筆札。

translations.literal: The emperor approved and ordered the Secretary to provide brushes and writing tablets.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor granted his request and ordered the Imperial Secretary to furnish him with brushes and writing tablets.

sentences.zh: 相如以「子虛」，虛言也，為楚稱；

translations.literal: Xiangru used 'Sir Fantasy' — meaning empty words — to speak for Chu;
translations.idiomatic: Xiangru devised 'Sir Fantasy' — a name meaning 'empty talk' — as the spokesman for Chu;

sentences.zh: 「烏有先生」者，烏有此事也，為齊難；

translations.literal: 'Master No-Such' — meaning 'how could such a thing exist' — to raise objections for Qi;
translations.idiomatic: 'Master No-Such' — meaning 'how could such a thing be' — to argue on behalf of Qi;

sentences.zh: 「無是公」者，無是人也，明天子之義。

translations.literal: 'Lord No-One' — meaning 'there is no such person' — to illuminate the righteousness of the Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: and 'Lord No-One' — meaning 'no such person exists' — to illuminate the grandeur and virtue of the Son of Heaven.

sentences.zh: 故空藉此三人為辭，以推天子諸侯之苑囿。

translations.literal: Thus he invented these three characters as a literary device, in order to compare the parks and hunting grounds of the Son of Heaven and the feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: He thus invented these three fictional characters as a literary conceit, using them to compare the parks and hunting grounds of the Son of Heaven with those of the feudal lords.

sentences.zh: 其卒章歸之於節儉，因以風諫。

translations.literal: The concluding section returns to frugality and restraint, thereby offering oblique admonition.
translations.idiomatic: The final section circles back to the virtues of frugality, delivering its admonition through allegory.

sentences.zh: 奏之天子，天子大說。

translations.literal: He presented it to the Son of Heaven, and the Son of Heaven was greatly pleased.
translations.idiomatic: When the rhapsody was presented to the emperor, the Son of Heaven was greatly pleased.

sentences.zh: 其辭曰：

translations.literal: Its text reads:
translations.idiomatic: The text reads as follows:

sentences.zh: 楚使子虛使於齊，齊王悉發境內之士，備車騎之眾，與使者出田。

translations.literal: Chu sent Sir Fantasy as envoy to Qi. The King of Qi mobilized all the warriors within his borders, assembled a great host of chariots and horsemen, and went hunting with the envoy.
translations.idiomatic: The kingdom of Chu dispatched Sir Fantasy as its envoy to Qi. The King of Qi mustered every warrior in his realm, marshaled a vast host of chariots and cavalry, and rode out to hunt with the envoy.

sentences.zh: 田罷，子虛過詫烏有先生，而無是公在焉。

translations.literal: When the hunt was over, Sir Fantasy visited Master No-Such, and Lord No-One was also present.
translations.idiomatic: When the hunt was finished, Sir Fantasy paid a call on Master No-Such, and Lord No-One happened to be there as well.

sentences.zh: 坐定，烏有先生問曰：「今日田樂乎？」

translations.literal: When they were seated, Master No-Such asked: 'Was today's hunt enjoyable?'
translations.idiomatic: Once they had settled into their seats, Master No-Such asked, 'Did you enjoy today's hunt?'

sentences.zh: 子虛曰：「樂。」

translations.literal: Sir Fantasy said: 'Enjoyable.'
translations.idiomatic: Sir Fantasy replied, 'Very much.'

sentences.zh: 「獲多乎？」

translations.literal: 'Was the catch plentiful?'
translations.idiomatic: 'Was the catch plentiful?'

translations.literal: He said: 'Meager.'
translations.idiomatic: 'Rather meager,' he said.

sentences.zh: 「然則何樂？」

translations.literal: 'Then what was enjoyable about it?'
translations.idiomatic: 'Then what did you enjoy about it?'

sentences.zh: 曰：「仆樂齊王之欲夸仆以車騎之眾，而仆對以雲夢之事也。」

translations.literal: He said: 'I enjoyed that the King of Qi wanted to impress me with his host of chariots and riders, while I responded with the wonders of Yunmeng.'
translations.idiomatic: 'What I enjoyed,' he said, 'was that the King of Qi tried to overawe me with the splendor of his cavalry, and I countered with an account of the glories of Yunmeng.'

sentences.zh: 曰：「可得聞乎？」

translations.literal: He said: 'May I hear about it?'
translations.idiomatic: 'May I hear the tale?' asked Master No-Such.

sentences.zh: 子虛曰：「可。

translations.literal: Sir Fantasy said: 'Certainly.
translations.idiomatic: Sir Fantasy said, 'Of course.

sentences.zh: 王駕車千乘，選徒萬騎，田於海濱。

translations.literal: The king drove a thousand chariots, selected ten thousand mounted soldiers, and hunted by the seashore.
translations.idiomatic: The king rode out with a thousand chariots and ten thousand picked cavalry, hunting along the seashore.

sentences.zh: 列卒滿澤，罘罔彌山，揜兔轔鹿，射麋腳鱗。

translations.literal: Ranks of soldiers filled the marshes, nets and snares covered the mountains; they caught rabbits and ran over deer, shot elk and trampled the scaled beasts.
translations.idiomatic: Ranks of soldiers filled the marshes; nets and snares blanketed the hills. They caught hares and trampled deer, shot elk and trod upon the scaled creatures.

sentences.zh: 騖於鹽浦，割鮮染輪射中獲多，顧謂仆曰：『楚亦有平原廣澤游獵之地饒樂若此者乎？

translations.literal: They galloped through the salt flats, cutting fresh meat until the blood stained the chariot wheels, the kills many from so many hits. He turned to me and said: 'Does Chu also have plains and broad marshes with hunting grounds as abundant and joyful as these?
translations.idiomatic: They raced across the salt flats, carving fresh game until blood stained the chariot wheels. The kill was enormous. The king turned to me and asked, 'Does Chu possess any plains or vast marshlands with hunting grounds as rich and pleasurable as these?

sentences.zh: 楚王之獵何與寡人？』

translations.literal: How does the King of Chu's hunt compare with mine?'
translations.idiomatic: How does the King of Chu's hunt compare to mine?'

sentences.zh: 仆下車對曰：『臣，楚國之鄙人也，幸得宿衛十有餘年，時從出游，游於後園，覽於有無，然猶未能遍覩也，又惡足以言其外澤者乎！』

translations.literal: I descended from the chariot and replied: 'Your servant is merely a humble man from Chu who was fortunate enough to serve as a palace guard for more than ten years, sometimes accompanying excursions in the rear gardens, surveying what was there and what was not, yet I was still unable to see it all — how could I presume to speak of the outer marshlands!'
translations.idiomatic: I descended from the chariot and replied, 'Your servant is but a humble man from Chu who was fortunate enough to serve in the palace guard for more than ten years. From time to time I accompanied excursions through the rear gardens, catching glimpses here and there, yet I never managed to see the whole of it — how could I presume to speak of the vast marshlands beyond!'

sentences.zh: 齊王曰：『雖然，略以子之所聞見而言之。』

translations.literal: The King of Qi said: 'Even so, speak roughly of what you have heard and seen.'
translations.idiomatic: The King of Qi said, 'Be that as it may, tell me broadly what you have heard and seen.'

sentences.zh: 「仆對曰：『唯唯。

translations.literal: I replied: 'Very well.
translations.idiomatic: I replied, 'As you wish.

sentences.zh: 臣聞楚有七澤，嘗見其一，未覩其余也。

translations.literal: Your servant has heard that Chu has seven great marshes; I have seen but one of them and have not beheld the rest.
translations.idiomatic: I have heard that Chu possesses seven great marshlands. I have seen only one, and have never laid eyes on the rest.

sentences.zh: 臣之所見，蓋特其小小者耳，名曰雲夢。

translations.literal: What your servant has seen is merely the smallest of them, called Yunmeng.
translations.idiomatic: The one I saw is merely the smallest among them. It is called Yunmeng.

sentences.zh: 雲夢者，方九百里，其中有山焉。

translations.literal: Yunmeng is nine hundred li on each side, and within it there are mountains.
translations.idiomatic: Yunmeng stretches nine hundred li in every direction, and within its expanse rise mountains.

sentences.zh: 其山則盤紆岪郁，隆崇嵂崒；

translations.literal: Its mountains are winding and luxuriantly dense, towering lofty and steep;
translations.idiomatic: Those mountains coil and twist in dense profusion, soaring high and sheer;

sentences.zh: 岑巖參差，日月蔽虧；

translations.literal: Rocky peaks rise in uneven array, veiling and eclipsing the sun and moon;
translations.idiomatic: their craggy peaks jut skyward in jagged ranks, veiling the sun and eclipsing the moon;

sentences.zh: 交錯糾紛，上干青雲；

translations.literal: They cross and tangle together, reaching up to the blue clouds;
translations.idiomatic: they interweave and tangle one upon another, thrusting upward into the blue clouds;

sentences.zh: 罷池陂陁，下屬江河。

translations.literal: Slopes and embankments, undulating terrain — below they connect to the great rivers.
translations.idiomatic: their slopes and ridges roll and undulate downward until they reach the rivers below.

sentences.zh: 其土則丹青赭堊，雌黃白坿，錫碧金銀，眾色炫燿，照爛龍鱗。

translations.literal: The soil includes vermilion, azurite, ochre, chalk, orpiment, whiteite, tin, jade-green, gold, and silver — a multitude of colors dazzling and blazing, shining brilliantly like dragon scales.
translations.idiomatic: The earth holds vermilion and azurite, ochre and chalk, orpiment and gypsum, tin and jade-green, gold and silver — a riot of colors dazzling and blazing, shimmering like the scales of a dragon.

sentences.zh: 其石則赤玉玫瑰，琳瑉琨珸，瑊玏玄厲，瑌石武夫。

translations.literal: Its stones include red jade and rose-colored gems, fine jade and variegated stone, dark whetstones and black flint, lustrous stones and warrior stone.
translations.idiomatic: Its stones include crimson jade and rose quartz, lustrous gems and variegated marble, dark whetstones and black flint, gleaming minerals and warrior stone.

sentences.zh: 其東則有蕙圃衡蘭，芷若射干，穹窮昌蒲，江離麋蕪，諸蔗猼且。

translations.literal: To the east there are gardens of orchids and fragrant thoroughwort, angelica and pollia, blackberry lily, acorus calamus, lovage and ligusticum, sugarcane and baiqie grass.
translations.idiomatic: To the east lie gardens of orchid and thoroughwort, angelica and pollia, iris and sweet flag, lovage and ligusticum, sugarcane and fragrant grasses.

sentences.zh: 其南則有平原廣澤，登降陁靡，案衍壇曼，緣以大江，限以巫山。

translations.literal: To the south there are level plains and broad marshes, with rising and falling, sloping and spreading terrain, bordered by the great river and bounded by Mount Wu.
translations.idiomatic: To the south stretch level plains and broad marshlands, their terrain rising and falling in gentle undulations, bordered by the Great River and bounded by the peaks of Mount Wu.

sentences.zh: 其高燥則生葴簛苞荔，薛莎青薠。

translations.literal: On the high, dry ground grow thorny shrubs, marsh grass, plantain, and green sedge.
translations.idiomatic: On the high, dry ground grow thorny shrubs and marsh grass, plantain and green sedge.

sentences.zh: 其卑溼則生藏莨蒹葭，東薔雕胡，蓮藕菰蘆，蔄軒芋，眾物居之，不可勝圖。

translations.literal: In the low, marshy areas grow ranunculus and reeds, wild rice and water plants, lotus roots and wild rice, taro and various vegetation — so many things dwell there they cannot all be depicted.
translations.idiomatic: In the low, marshy ground grow ranunculus and reeds, wild rice and water plants, lotus roots and cattails, taro and countless other species — so numerous that no inventory could capture them all.

sentences.zh: 其西則有湧泉清池，激水推移；

translations.literal: To the west there are gushing springs and clear pools, with surging waters pushing and flowing;
translations.idiomatic: To the west lie gushing springs and crystal pools, their waters surging and flowing;

sentences.zh: 外發芙蓉蔆華，內隱鉅石白沙。

translations.literal: On the surface bloom lotus blossoms and water caltrop flowers; below lie hidden great boulders and white sand.
translations.idiomatic: on their surface bloom lotus blossoms and water caltrop flowers, while beneath lie hidden great boulders and white sand.

sentences.zh: 其中則有神龜蛟鼉，瑁鱉黿。

translations.literal: Within them dwell divine turtles, flood dragons, alligators, hawksbill sea turtles, soft-shell turtles, and great river turtles.
translations.idiomatic: Within these waters dwell divine tortoises and flood dragons, crocodiles, hawksbill turtles, soft-shell turtles, and giant river turtles.

sentences.zh: 其北則有陰林巨樹，楩枏豫章，桂椒木蘭，蘗離朱楊，櫨梸梬栗，橘柚芬芳。

translations.literal: To the north there are shady forests and giant trees: phoebe, camphor, cassia, pepper tree, magnolia, cork tree, cherry, red willow, sumac, persimmon, chestnut, and the fragrance of tangerines and pomelos.
translations.idiomatic: To the north stand shadowed forests of towering trees — phoebe and camphor, cassia and pepper, magnolia and cork, cherry and red willow, sumac and persimmon, chestnut, and the sweet fragrance of tangerines and pomelos.

sentences.zh: 其上則有赤猨蠷蝚，鹓雛孔鸞，騰遠射干。

translations.literal: Above dwell red apes and long-armed gibbons, phoenixes and peacocks, soaring afar and leaping high.
translations.idiomatic: In the canopy above dwell red apes and long-armed gibbons, phoenixes and peacocks, soaring far and leaping among the branches.

sentences.zh: 其下則有白虎玄豹，蟃蜒貙豻，兕象野犀，窮奇獌狿。

translations.literal: Below there are white tigers and black leopards, giant serpents and lynxes, rhinoceroses and elephants, wild rhinos, and the mythical beasts qiongqi and manluo.
translations.idiomatic: On the ground below roam white tigers and black leopards, giant serpents and lynxes, rhinoceroses and elephants, wild rhinos, and the fabled beasts qiongqi and manluo.

sentences.zh: 「『於是乃使專諸之倫，手格此獸。

translations.literal: Thereupon warriors of Zhuan Zhu's caliber were sent to grapple with these beasts bare-handed.
translations.idiomatic: And so warriors of the caliber of Zhuan Zhu were dispatched to wrestle these beasts with their bare hands.

sentences.zh: 楚王乃駕馴駁之駟，乘雕玉之輿，靡魚須之橈旃，曳明月之珠旗，建干將之雄戟，左烏嗥之雕弓，右夏服之勁箭；

translations.literal: The King of Chu then drove a team of dappled chariot horses, rode in a chariot carved with jade, unfurled banners of sharkskin, trailed pennants studded with pearls bright as the moon, raised a great halberd forged by Ganjiang, carried on his left a carved bow from Wuhao, and on his right powerful arrows from the Xia quiver;
translations.idiomatic: The King of Chu then drove a team of dappled chargers, riding in a jade-carved chariot. He unfurled banners trimmed with sharkskin, trailed pennants studded with pearls bright as the moon, raised a great halberd forged by the legendary smith Ganjiang, carried on his left a carved bow of Wuhao, and on his right powerful arrows from the Xia quiver;

sentences.zh: 陽子驂乘，纖阿為御；

translations.literal: Lord Yang rode as outrider, and Xian'e served as charioteer;
translations.idiomatic: Lord Yang rode as his outrider, and the master charioteer Xian'e held the reins;

sentences.zh: 案節未舒，即陵狡獸，轔邛邛，蹵距虛，軼野馬而韢騊駼，乘遺風而射游騏；

translations.literal: Before the pace was even fully set, they charged the cunning beasts, trampled the qiongqiong beast, kicked the juqu creature, overtook the wild horses and lassoed the taotu, rode the swift wind and shot the roaming unicorns;
translations.idiomatic: before the pace was even fully set, they charged into the cunning beasts, trampling the qiongqiong, kicking aside the juqu, outrunning wild horses and lassoing the taotu, riding the wind itself to shoot the roaming unicorns;

sentences.zh: 儵眒凄浰，雷動熛至，星流霆擊，弓不虛發，中必決眥，洞胸達腋，絕乎心系，獲若雨獸，揜草蔽地。

translations.literal: Swift and sudden, cold and fierce, moving like thunder and arriving like flying sparks, streaming like stars and striking like thunderbolts — no arrow loosed in vain, each hit splitting the eye socket, piercing through chest to armpit, severing the ligaments of the heart. The killed beasts fell like rain, covering the grass and blanketing the ground.
translations.idiomatic: Swift as a flash, fierce as the wind — they moved like thunder and struck like flying sparks, streaming like comets and crashing like thunderbolts. No arrow was loosed in vain: each one split an eye socket, pierced from chest to armpit, severed the very ligaments of the heart. Slain beasts fell like rain, carpeting the grass and blanketing the earth.

sentences.zh: 於是楚王乃弭節裴回，翱翔容與，覽乎陰林，觀壯士之暴怒，與猛獸之恐懼，徼劇受詘，殫睹眾物之變態。

translations.literal: Thereupon the King of Chu slowed his chariot and lingered, soaring leisurely about, surveying the shadowy forests, watching the warriors rage and the fierce beasts cower in terror, witnessing fierce clashes and yielding, exhaustively beholding the myriad transformations of all creatures.
translations.idiomatic: Then the King of Chu reined in his chariot and lingered, gliding at leisure to survey the shadowed forests. He watched his warriors rage and the fierce beasts cower, witnessed savage clashes and desperate surrenders, and took in the myriad transformations of every creature.

sentences.zh: 「『於是鄭女曼姬，被阿錫，揄紵縞，纖羅，垂霧縠；

translations.literal: Thereupon, the beauties of Zheng, draped in fine silk from E and tin-threaded cloth, trailing ramie and white crepe, wearing delicate gauze and letting hang misty crêpe;
translations.idiomatic: Then came the beauties of Zheng, draped in fine Esilk and lustrous cloth, trailing robes of ramie and white crepe, wearing gossamer gauze with wisps of misty crêpe;

sentences.zh: 襞積褰縐，紆徐委曲，郁橈谿谷；

translations.literal: Their garments folded and gathered, creased and crinkled, winding slowly in sinuous curves, filling every ravine and valley;
translations.idiomatic: their garments folded and gathered in flowing creases, winding in sinuous curves that filled every hollow and ravine;

sentences.zh: 衯衯裶裶，揚袘卹削，蜚纖垂髾；

translations.literal: Fluttering and billowing, their trailing hems rising and falling, fine streamers floating and tassels hanging;
translations.idiomatic: their hems fluttered and billowed, rising and dipping, while fine streamers floated aloft and tassels swayed;

sentences.zh: 扶與猗靡，膳萃蔡，下摩蘭蕙，上拂羽蓋，錯翡翠之威蕤，繆繞玉綏；

translations.literal: Swaying and leaning gracefully, brushing against the orchids and thoroughwort below, sweeping the feathered canopy above, with kingfisher plumes interwoven in splendid tassels, jade pendants winding about them;
translations.idiomatic: they swayed and leaned with supple grace, brushing orchids and thoroughwort below, sweeping the feathered canopy above, their robes interwoven with kingfisher plumes in splendid tassels, jade pendants winding about them;

sentences.zh: 縹乎忽忽，若神僊之仿佛。

translations.literal: Dim and indistinct, as if immortal spirits were hovering in a vision.
translations.idiomatic: ethereal and indistinct, as though immortal beings had descended in a vision.

sentences.zh: 「『於是乃相與獠於蕙圃，媻珊勃窣上金隄，揜翡翠，射鵕鸃，微矰出，纖繳施，弋白鵠，連駕鵝，雙鶬下，玄鶴加。

translations.literal: Thereupon they went hunting together in the orchid gardens, sauntering and rustling up the golden embankment, catching kingfishers, shooting the junyi bird, sending out tiny pellet arrows, casting fine tethered lines, shooting at white swans, linking wild geese in flight, bringing down pairs of cang birds, and adding black cranes to the catch.
translations.idiomatic: Then they hunted together in the orchid gardens, sauntering up the golden embankment. They caught kingfishers and shot the junyi bird, sent tiny pellet arrows flying and cast fine tethered lines, shot white swans and linked wild geese in flight, brought down pairs of cang birds and added black cranes to the catch.

sentences.zh: 怠而後發，游於清池；

translations.literal: When weary of this, they set forth and drifted upon the clear pools;
translations.idiomatic: When they tired of this, they set off to drift across the clear pools;

sentences.zh: 浮文鹢，揚桂枻，張翠帷，建羽蓋，罔瑁，釣紫貝；

translations.literal: They floated on painted cormorant-prowed boats, raised oars of cassia wood, spread kingfisher-green curtains, erected feathered canopies, netted hawksbill turtles, and angled for purple cowrie shells;
translations.idiomatic: they floated on painted cormorant-prowed boats, raised oars of cassia wood, spread kingfisher-green curtains, and erected feathered canopies. They netted hawksbill turtles and angled for purple cowrie shells;

sentences.zh: 摐金鼓，吹鳴籟，榜人歌，聲流喝，水蟲駭，波鴻沸，涌泉起，奔揚會，礧石相擊，硠硠礚礚，若雷霆之聲，聞乎數百里之外。

translations.literal: They struck bronze drums, blew singing pipes; the boatmen sang, their voices flowing and resounding; the water creatures were startled, the waves boiled and surged, springs erupted, torrents rushed and converged, boulders crashed together — clang and crash — the sound like thunder, heard hundreds of li away.
translations.idiomatic: They struck bronze drums and blew singing pipes. The boatmen sang, their voices carrying and resounding across the water. Aquatic creatures fled in alarm; the waves boiled and surged; springs erupted and torrents rushed together; boulders crashed against one another with a thunderous clangor that could be heard hundreds of li away.

sentences.zh: 「『將息獠者，擊靈鼓，起烽燧，車案行，騎就隊，纚乎淫淫，班乎裔裔。

translations.literal: When it was time to end the hunt, they struck the spirit drums, lit the signal fires; the chariots proceeded in order, the cavalry formed ranks, streaming along steadily, arrayed in grand procession.
translations.idiomatic: When it was time to end the hunt, they struck the spirit drums and lit the signal fires. The chariots advanced in formation, the cavalry fell into ranks — a vast procession streaming along in stately array.

sentences.zh: 於是楚王乃登陽雲之臺，泊乎無為，澹乎自持，勺藥之和具而後御之。

translations.literal: Thereupon the King of Chu ascended the Terrace of Sunlit Clouds, calm in non-action, tranquil in self-possession, and only after harmonious seasonings of peony broth were prepared did he partake.
translations.idiomatic: Then the King of Chu ascended the Terrace of Sunlit Clouds. There he sat in calm repose, at ease and self-possessed, and only after dishes had been harmoniously seasoned with peony broth did he deign to eat.

sentences.zh: 不若大王終日馳騁而不下輿，脟割輪淬，自以為娛。

translations.literal: This is unlike Your Majesty, who races all day without descending from the chariot, slicing raw meat on the wheel rim and dipping it — considering that entertainment.
translations.idiomatic: This is not like Your Majesty, who races about all day without descending from the chariot, slicing raw meat on the wheel rim and dipping it in sauce — and calling that entertainment.

sentences.zh: 臣竊觀之，齊殆不如。』

translations.literal: Your servant privately observes: Qi is nearly no match for it.'
translations.idiomatic: Your servant's private observation is that Qi can hardly compare.'

sentences.zh: 於是王默然無以應仆也。」

translations.literal: At this the king fell silent, with nothing to say in reply to me."
translations.idiomatic: At that, the king fell silent, having no reply to offer me."

sentences.zh: 烏有先生曰：「是何言之過也！

translations.literal: Master No-Such said: 'What an outrageous thing to say!
translations.idiomatic: Master No-Such exclaimed, 'What an absurd thing to say!

sentences.zh: 足下不遠千里，來況齊國，王悉發境內之士，而備車騎之眾，以出田，乃欲力致獲，以娛左右也，何名為夸哉！

translations.literal: You traveled a thousand li to honor Qi with your presence. The king mobilized all the warriors of his realm and assembled a great host of chariots and riders to go hunting, intending to exert himself to make a catch and entertain his attendants — how can this be called showing off!
translations.idiomatic: You traveled a thousand li to grace Qi with your presence. The king mustered every warrior in his realm and assembled a vast host of chariots and cavalry for the hunt, seeking only to bring in a good catch and entertain his companions — how is that showing off!

sentences.zh: 問楚地之有無者，願聞大國之風烈，先生之餘論也。

translations.literal: Asking about the features of Chu's territory was a wish to hear about the customs and glory of a great state — merely a topic of conversation, sir.
translations.idiomatic: When he asked about the features of Chu, he wished to hear about the customs and splendor of a great state — it was merely a topic for conversation, sir.

sentences.zh: 今足下不稱楚王之德厚，而盛推雲夢以為高，奢言淫樂而顯侈靡，竊為足下不取也。

translations.literal: Now instead of praising the virtue and benevolence of the King of Chu, you grandly promote Yunmeng as something lofty, speak extravagantly of indulgent pleasures and flaunt excess and waste — I privately consider this unworthy of you.
translations.idiomatic: Yet instead of praising the King of Chu's virtue and benevolence, you extol Yunmeng as something grand, speak extravagantly of indulgent pleasures, and parade excess and waste — I cannot approve of this.

sentences.zh: 必若所言，固非楚國之美也。

translations.literal: Even if it is as you say, this is hardly the glory of Chu.
translations.idiomatic: Even if all you say is true, this is hardly what makes Chu great.

sentences.zh: 有而言之，是章君之惡；

translations.literal: If it exists and you speak of it, you expose your lord's faults;
translations.idiomatic: If such things truly exist and you boast of them, you expose your lord's faults;

sentences.zh: 無而言之，是害足下之信。

translations.literal: If it does not exist and you speak of it, you damage your own credibility.
translations.idiomatic: if they do not exist and you boast of them, you damage your own credibility.

sentences.zh: 章君之惡而傷私義，二者無一可，而先生行之，必且輕於齊而累於楚矣。

translations.literal: Exposing your lord's faults and injuring personal duty — neither of these is acceptable, yet you do both. You will surely be belittled in Qi and burdened with blame in Chu.
translations.idiomatic: Exposing your lord's faults and betraying personal duty — neither is acceptable, and yet you have done both. You will surely be belittled in Qi and laden with blame in Chu.

sentences.zh: 且齊東陼巨海，南有瑯邪，觀乎成山，射乎之罘，浮勃澥，游孟諸，邪與肅慎為鄰，右以湯谷為界，秋田乎青丘，傍偟乎海外，吞若雲夢者八九，其於胸中曾不蔕芥。

translations.literal: Moreover, Qi borders the great sea to the east, has Langya to the south, surveys Mount Cheng, hunts at Zhifu, floats on the Bohai, roams Mengzhu, neighbors the Sushen to the northeast, and is bounded by Tanggu to the right. In autumn it hunts at Qingqiu, and wanders beyond the seas — it could swallow eight or nine Yunmengs without even noticing them in its breast.
translations.idiomatic: Moreover, Qi borders the great sea to the east, possesses Langya to the south, surveys Mount Cheng and hunts at Zhifu, floats upon the Bohai and roams the Mengzhu marshes, neighbors the Sushen in the northeast and is bounded by Tanggu to the west. In autumn it hunts at Qingqiu and wanders beyond the seas — it could swallow eight or nine Yunmengs without so much as noticing them.

sentences.zh: 若乃俶儻瑰偉，異方殊類，珍怪鳥獸，萬端鱗萃，充仞其中者，不可勝記，禹不能名，契不能計。

translations.literal: As for the extraordinary and magnificent, the strange regions and unusual species, the rare and wondrous birds and beasts gathered in myriad varieties that fill its expanse — they cannot all be recorded. Yu could not name them, and Xie could not count them.
translations.idiomatic: As for the extraordinary and magnificent, the strange regions and singular species, the rare and wondrous birds and beasts gathered in myriad varieties that fill its vastness — they defy enumeration. Even the sage Yu could not name them all, nor could Xie tally them.

sentences.zh: 然在諸侯之位，不敢言游戲之樂，苑囿之大；

translations.literal: Yet being in the position of a feudal lord, the king does not dare to speak of the pleasures of excursions or the vastness of his parks;
translations.idiomatic: Yet because he holds the rank of a feudal lord, the king does not presume to boast of the pleasures of his excursions or the vastness of his parks;

sentences.zh: 先生又見客，是以王辭而不復，何為無用應哉！」

translations.literal: Moreover, you were a guest, and so the king declined to reply in kind — how can you say he had no answer!'
translations.idiomatic: and moreover, you were a guest, so the king politely declined to respond in kind. How can you say he had no answer!'

sentences.zh: 無是公聽然而笑曰：「楚則失矣，齊亦未為得也。

translations.literal: Lord No-One laughed broadly and said: 'Chu is in the wrong, but Qi has not gotten it right either.
translations.idiomatic: Lord No-One laughed aloud and said, 'Chu is in the wrong, but Qi has not gotten it right either.

sentences.zh: 夫使諸侯納貢者，非為財幣，所以述職也；

translations.literal: The purpose of having feudal lords pay tribute is not for the sake of wealth and goods, but to allow them to report on their duties;
translations.idiomatic: The purpose of requiring feudal lords to pay tribute is not to collect wealth, but to have them report on their duties;

sentences.zh: 封疆畫界者，非為守御，所以禁淫也。

translations.literal: The purpose of establishing domains and drawing boundaries is not for defense, but to restrain excess.
translations.idiomatic: the purpose of establishing domains and drawing boundaries is not for defense, but to restrain excess.

sentences.zh: 今齊列為東藩，而外私肅慎，捐國踰限，越海而田，其於義故未可也。

translations.literal: Now Qi is ranked as an eastern vassal state, yet it privately consorts with the Sushen abroad, abandons its own territory and transgresses its boundaries, crossing the seas to hunt — this is improper by any standard of duty.
translations.idiomatic: Qi is ranked as a vassal of the eastern marches, yet it privately consorts with the Sushen abroad, neglects its own territory, transgresses its boundaries, and crosses the seas to hunt — all of which violates the proper order of duty.

sentences.zh: 且二君之論，不務明君臣之義而正諸侯之禮，徒事爭游獵之樂，苑囿之大，欲以奢侈相勝，荒淫相越，此不可以揚名發譽，而適足以貶君自損也。

translations.literal: Moreover, in the debate between you two, you do not strive to clarify the duty between lord and subject or to uphold the ritual propriety of feudal lords. You merely compete over the pleasures of hunting and the grandeur of parks, trying to outdo each other in extravagance and excess. This cannot bring fame and renown, but only serves to diminish your lords and damage yourselves.
translations.idiomatic: And in this debate between the two of you, neither has sought to clarify the duties between lord and subject or uphold the proper rites of the feudal lords. You merely compete over the pleasures of the hunt and the grandeur of your parks, trying to outdo each other in extravagance and excess. Far from bringing fame or renown, this serves only to diminish your lords and damage yourselves.

sentences.zh: 且夫齊楚之事又焉足道邪！

translations.literal: Besides, the affairs of Qi and Chu — are they even worth discussing!
translations.idiomatic: And besides, are the affairs of Qi and Chu even worth discussing!

sentences.zh: 君未睹夫巨麗也，獨不聞天子之上林乎？

translations.literal: You have not yet beheld true magnificence — have you never heard of the Son of Heaven's Shanglin Park?
translations.idiomatic: You have not yet beheld true magnificence. Have you never heard of the Son of Heaven's Shanglin Park?

sentences.zh: 「左蒼梧，右西極，丹水更其南，紫淵徑其北；

translations.literal: To the left lies Cangwu, to the right the Western Extremity; the Cinnabar River runs along its south, the Purple Abyss passes through its north;
translations.idiomatic: To the left lies Cangwu, to the right the Western Extremity. The Cinnabar River traces its southern boundary, and the Purple Abyss courses through its north;

sentences.zh: 終始霸滻，出入涇渭；

translations.literal: It encompasses the Ba and Chan Rivers from end to end, and the Jing and Wei Rivers flow in and out of it;
translations.idiomatic: the Ba and Chan rivers run through it from end to end, while the Jing and Wei flow in and out;

sentences.zh: 酆鄗潦潏，紆餘委蛇，經營乎其內。

translations.literal: The Feng, the Hao, the Lao, and the Jue wind and meander, twisting and turning through its interior.
translations.idiomatic: the Feng, the Hao, the Lao, and the Jue wind and meander in serpentine courses through its interior.

sentences.zh: 蕩蕩兮八川分流，相背而異態。

translations.literal: Vast and expansive, the eight rivers flow apart, diverging in different directions and forms.
translations.idiomatic: Vast and expansive, the eight rivers branch and flow apart, each diverging on its own course and taking its own form.

sentences.zh: 東西南北，馳騖往來，出乎椒丘之闕，行乎洲淤之浦，徑乎桂林之中，過乎泱莽之野。

translations.literal: East and west, south and north, they race and rush back and forth, issuing from the passes of Pepper Hill, flowing through the shores of alluvial islands, passing through the cassia groves, and crossing the vast open wilderness.
translations.idiomatic: In every direction they race and rush: issuing from the passes of Pepper Hill, flowing past the alluvial shores, threading through groves of cassia, and crossing the vast open wilderness.

sentences.zh: 汨乎渾流，順阿而下，赴隘陜之口。

translations.literal: Surging in turbid streams, flowing along the bends downward, rushing toward narrow gorge mouths.
translations.idiomatic: They surge in turbid torrents, follow the bends downhill, and rush toward the mouths of narrow gorges.

sentences.zh: 觸穹石，激堆埼，沸乎暴怒，洶涌滂撓，蜿灗膠戾，湢測泌瀄，橫流逆折，轉騰潎洌，澎濞沆瀣，穹隆雲撓，蜿灗膠戾，踰波趨浥，蒞蒞下瀨，批壧衝壅，奔揚滯沛，臨坻注壑，瀺灂霣墜，湛湛隱隱，砰磅訇礚，潏潏淈淈，湁潗鼎沸，馳波跳沫，汩急漂疾，悠遠長懷，寂漻無聲，肆乎永歸。

translations.literal: Dashing against arched boulders, striking heaped promontories, seething and raging, surging and roiling, coiling and twisting, seeping and percolating, flowing crossways and folding back, tumbling and leaping in clear jets, booming and spraying, arching and swirling, winding and churning, cresting waves and skimming shallows, cascading down rapids, cleaving cliffs and smashing dams, rushing and surging in torrential floods, plunging from banks into ravines, splashing and crashing downward, deep and dark, rumbling and thundering, gushing and bubbling, roiling like a cauldron, racing in waves and leaping in foam, rushing swift and floating fast, then far and wide with lingering calm, silent and still, at last flowing to its eternal destination.
translations.idiomatic: They dash against arched boulders and strike heaped promontories, seething and raging, surging and roiling. They coil and twist, seep and percolate, flow crossways and fold back, tumble and leap in crystal jets, boom and spray, arch high like clouds and swirl. They coil again and churn, crest waves and skim the shallows, cascade down rapids, cleave cliffs and smash through dams, rush and surge in torrential floods, plunge from banks into ravines, splash and crash downward. Deep and dark, they rumble and thunder, gush and bubble, roil like a cauldron, race in waves and leap in foam, rush swift and flow fast — then stretch far and wide in lingering calm, falling silent and still, flowing at last to their eternal destination.

sentences.zh: 然後灝溔潢漾，安翔徐徊，翯乎滈滈，東注大湖，衍溢陂池。

translations.literal: Then they spread vast and rippling, gliding peacefully and slowly circling, gleaming and shimmering, flowing east into the great lake, overflowing into the ponds and embankments.
translations.idiomatic: Then they spread out, vast and rippling, gliding peacefully and circling slowly, gleaming and shimmering, flowing eastward into the great lake, overflowing into ponds and embankments.

sentences.zh: 於是乎蛟龍赤螭，鯁鰽螹離，鰅鳙鯱魠，禺禺鱋魶，揵鰭擢尾，振鱗奮翼，潛處于深巖；

translations.literal: Therein dwell flood dragons and scarlet serpents, sturgeon and lampreys, catfish and sharks, strange fish of all kinds, raising their fins and extending their tails, shaking their scales and flapping their wings, lurking deep among the submerged rocks;
translations.idiomatic: There dwell flood dragons and scarlet serpents, sturgeon and lampreys, catfish and sharks, and strange fish of every kind — raising their fins and extending their tails, shaking their scales and flapping their wing-like fins, lurking in the depths among submerged rocks;

sentences.zh: 魚鱉讙聲，萬物眾夥，明月珠子，玓瓅江靡，蜀石黃碝，水玉磊砢，磷磷爛爛，采色澔旰，叢積乎其中。

translations.literal: Fish and turtles cry out in chorus; a multitude of creatures throng together. Moon-bright pearls gleam and shimmer amid the waves; Shu stone, yellow agate, and water jade lie heaped in clusters — sparkling and radiant, a blaze of colors dazzling and bright, all gathered within.
translations.idiomatic: fish and turtles raise a clamor; creatures teem by the myriad. Moon-bright pearls gleam and shimmer amid the waves; Shu stone, yellow agate, and water jade lie in heaped clusters — sparkling and radiant, a dazzling blaze of color, all gathered within.

sentences.zh: 鴻鵠鷫鴇，鴐鵝鸀玉，鵁鶄鹮目，煩鶩鷛鸜，鷻鴜鵁鸕，群浮乎其上。

translations.literal: Swans and wild geese, crested geese and bustards, domesticated geese and jade-green ducks, egrets and cranes, ibises and spoonbills, bustling ducks and mynah birds, pelicans and cormorants — flocks float upon the surface.
translations.idiomatic: Swans and wild geese, crested geese and bustards, domestic geese and jade-green ducks, egrets and cranes, ibises and spoonbills, bustling ducks and mynah birds, pelicans and cormorants — whole flocks float upon the surface.

sentences.zh: 汎淫泛濫，隨風澹淡，與波搖蕩，掩薄草渚，唼喋菁藻，咀嚼蔆藕。

translations.literal: They drift and spread, swaying gently with the wind, rocking with the waves, pressing against the grassy islets, dabbling at green algae, and chewing on water caltrop and lotus roots.
translations.idiomatic: They drift and spread across the water, swaying gently with the wind, rocking with the waves. They press against the grassy islets, dabble at green algae, and chew on water caltrop and lotus roots.

sentences.zh: 「於是乎崇山巃嵸，崔巍嵯峨，深林鉅木，嶄巖嵾嵯，九嵏、嶻嶭，南山峨峨，巖陁甗锜，嶊崣崛崎，振谿通谷，蹇產溝瀆，谽呀豁閜，阜陵別島，崴磈嵔瘣，丘虛崛礨，隱轔郁礨，登降施靡，陂池貏豸，沇溶淫鬻，散渙夷陸，亭皋千里，靡不被筑。

translations.literal: Then there are lofty mountains towering and majestic, sublime and precipitous, deep forests and giant trees, sheer cliffs and jagged crags, the nine peaks jutting up, the southern mountains soaring high, rock-strewn slopes and rugged terrain, undulating ridges, ravines opening into valleys, trenches and ditches, gaping chasms and yawning gorges, mounds and islands, craggy knolls, hillocks and tumuli, rumbling and massed, rising and falling in gentle slopes, embankments and pools, flowing and spreading, dispersing across level ground — for a thousand li of elevated marshland, nothing is left uncovered.
translations.idiomatic: Then there are the lofty mountains, towering and majestic, sublime and precipitous, with deep forests and giant trees, sheer cliffs and jagged crags. The nine peaks jut skyward; the southern mountains soar. Rock-strewn slopes and rugged terrain stretch on, undulating ridges fall away, ravines open into valleys, trenches and ditches cut the earth, and gaping chasms yawn wide. Mounds and islands, craggy knolls, hillocks and tumuli rise and fall in gentle slopes. Embankments and pools flow and spread, dispersing across level ground — for a thousand li of elevated marshland, nothing is left uncovered.

sentences.zh: 掩以綠蕙，被以江離，糅以蘪蕪，雜以流夷。

translations.literal: Covered with green orchids, blanketed with lovage, mixed with ligusticum, and mingled with gladiolus.
translations.idiomatic: The ground is covered with green orchids, blanketed with lovage, blended with ligusticum, and mingled with gladiolus.

sentences.zh: 專結縷，欑戾莎，揭車衡蘭，槁本射干，茈薑蘘荷，葴橙若蓀，鮮枝黃礫，蔣芧青薠，布濩閎澤，延曼太原，麗靡廣衍，應風披靡，吐芳揚烈，郁郁斐斐，眾香發越，肸蠁布寫，崦瞹苾勃。

translations.literal: Clover and knotgrass, clustered sedge, cart-vine and thoroughwort, lovage root and iris, purple ginger and wild ginger, thorny orange, pollia, fresh shoots and yellow pebbleplant, wild rice grass and green sedge — spread across the vast marshes, extending over the great plains, clinging beautifully in wide expanses, bending with the wind, exhaling fragrance and emitting perfume, rich and ornate, a multitude of scents rising and spreading, pervading and diffusing, thick and sweet.
translations.idiomatic: Clover and knotgrass, clustered sedge, cart-vine and thoroughwort, lovage root and iris, purple ginger and wild ginger, thorny orange and pollia, fresh shoots and yellow pebbleplant, wild rice grass and green sedge — they spread across the vast marshes and extend over the great plains, carpeting wide expanses in beauty. They bend with the wind, exhaling fragrance and emitting perfume, rich and ornate. A multitude of scents rise and spread, pervading and diffusing, thick and sweet.

sentences.zh: 「於是乎周覽泛觀，瞋盼軋沕，芒芒恍忽，視之無端，察之無崖。

translations.literal: Looking all around in a sweeping gaze, glancing this way and that, it stretches vast and indistinct — look, and there is no beginning; examine it, and there is no end.
translations.idiomatic: Gazing all around in a sweeping panorama, looking this way and that, the vista stretches vast and indistinct — look and there is no beginning; examine it and there is no end.

sentences.zh: 日出東沼，入於西陂。

translations.literal: The sun rises over the eastern marshes and sets behind the western pools.
translations.idiomatic: The sun rises over the eastern marshes and sets behind the western pools.

sentences.zh: 其南則隆冬生長，踴水躍波；

translations.literal: In its south, life flourishes even in the depths of winter, and waters surge and waves leap;
translations.idiomatic: In the south, life flourishes even in the depths of winter — waters surge and waves leap;

sentences.zh: 獸則庸獏牦犛，沈牛麈麋，赤首圜題，窮奇象犀。

translations.literal: The beasts include yong tapirs, yaks and musk oxen, water buffalo and moose, red-headed and round-browed creatures, the qiongqi, elephants, and rhinoceroses.
translations.idiomatic: its beasts include tapirs, yaks and musk oxen, water buffalo and moose, red-headed round-browed creatures, the mythical qiongqi, elephants, and rhinoceroses.

sentences.zh: 其北則盛夏含凍裂地，涉冰揭河；

translations.literal: In its north, even in midsummer the ground holds frozen earth and cracks with cold, ice covers the rivers and must be forded;
translations.idiomatic: In the north, even at midsummer the ground holds frozen earth and cracks with cold, and one must cross ice-covered rivers;

sentences.zh: 獸則麒麟角端，騊駼橐駞，蛩蛩驒騱，駃騠驢騾。

translations.literal: The beasts include the unicorn and the horned duan, the taotu and the camel, the qiongqiong and the tuoxi, the hinny, the donkey, and the mule.
translations.idiomatic: its beasts include the unicorn and the horned duan, the taotu and the camel, the qiongqiong and the tuoxi, hinnies, donkeys, and mules.

sentences.zh: 「於是乎離宮別館，彌山跨谷，高廊四注，重坐曲閣，華榱璧璫，輦道纚屬，步櫩周流，長途中宿。

translations.literal: Then there are detached palaces and separate lodges, covering the mountains and spanning the valleys, with lofty corridors pouring in four directions, tiered halls and winding pavilions, ornate rafters and jade pendants, imperial carriage-roads in unbroken succession, covered walkways circling about, with rest-stops along the long roads.
translations.idiomatic: Then there are the detached palaces and separate lodges, covering the mountains and spanning the valleys. Lofty corridors extend in four directions; tiered halls and winding pavilions rise on every side. Ornate rafters gleam with jade pendants, imperial carriage-roads stretch in unbroken succession, covered walkways circle around, and rest-stops line the long roads.

sentences.zh: 夷嵏筑堂，纍臺增成，巖穾洞房，俛杳眇而無見，仰攀橑而捫天，奔星更於閨闥，宛虹拖於楯軒。

translations.literal: They leveled peaks to build halls, heaped terrace upon terrace; in rocky alcoves and cavern chambers, one looks down into dimness beyond sight, and looks up to grasp the rafters and touch heaven. Shooting stars dart past the inner gates, and curving rainbows trail along the balustrades.
translations.idiomatic: Peaks were leveled to build halls, terraces heaped one upon another. In rocky alcoves and cavern chambers, one peers down into darkness beyond sight, and gazes up to grasp the rafters and touch the sky. Shooting stars dart past the inner gates, and curving rainbows trail along the balustrades.

sentences.zh: 青虯蚴蟉於東箱，象輿婉蟬於西清，靈圉燕於閒觀，偓佺之倫暴於南榮，醴泉涌於清室，通川過乎中庭。

translations.literal: Green dragons coil in the eastern chambers, elephant-drawn carriages glide gracefully in the western halls, spirit guardians feast in the leisure pavilions, immortals of the caliber of Wo Quan bask on the southern eaves, sweet springs gush in the clear chambers, and flowing streams pass through the central courtyard.
translations.idiomatic: Green dragons coil in the eastern chambers; elephant-drawn carriages glide through the western halls. Spirit guardians feast in the leisure pavilions, and immortals of Wo Quan's caliber bask on the southern eaves. Sweet springs gush in the clear chambers, and flowing streams pass through the central courtyard.

sentences.zh: 槃石裖崖，嵚巖倚傾，嵯峨磼礏，刻削崢嶸，玫瑰碧琳，珊瑚叢生，瑉玉旁唐，璸斒文鱗，赤瑕駁犖，雜臿其閒，垂綏琬琰，和氏出焉。

translations.literal: Massive boulders prop the cliffs, towering crags lean and tilt, lofty and precipitous, carved and sculpted into soaring forms. Rose quartz and green jade, corals growing in clusters, fine marble and jade arrayed in rows, patterned scales and mottled red flaws — all mixed and planted among them. Hanging pendants of fine jade — the stone of Master He himself came from here.
translations.idiomatic: Massive boulders prop the cliffs, and towering crags lean and tilt, lofty and precipitous, carved and sculpted into soaring forms. Rose quartz and green jade, corals growing in clusters, fine marble and jade arrayed in patterns — all are scattered among them. Hanging pendants of the finest jade sway, for the very stone of Master He came from here.

sentences.zh: 「於是乎盧橘夏孰，黃甘橙楱，枇杷橪柿，楟柰厚樸，梬棗楊梅，櫻桃蒲陶，隱夫郁棣，榙遝荔枝，羅乎后宮，列乎北園。

translations.literal: Then there are loquats ripening in summer, yellow citrus and oranges, limes, persimmons, crabapples and magnolia bark, jujubes and bayberries, cherries and grapes, silken cotton and arbor vitae, lychees — spread throughout the rear palace and arrayed in the northern gardens.
translations.idiomatic: Then there are loquats ripening in summer, yellow citrus and oranges and limes, persimmons, crabapples and magnolia bark, jujubes and bayberries, cherries and grapes, the silken cotton tree and arbor vitae, and lychees — all spread throughout the rear palace and arrayed in the northern gardens.

sentences.zh: 貤丘陵，下平原，揚翠葉，杌紫莖，發紅華，秀朱榮，煌煌扈扈，照曜鉅野。

translations.literal: Spreading over hills and descending to the plains, displaying emerald leaves and thick purple stems, bursting with crimson blossoms and brilliant scarlet flowers — dazzling and resplendent, illuminating the vast wilderness.
translations.idiomatic: They spread over the hills and descend to the plains, unfurling emerald leaves and thick purple stems, bursting with crimson blossoms and brilliant scarlet flowers — dazzling and resplendent, lighting up the vast wilderness.

sentences.zh: 沙棠櫟櫧，華氾檘櫨，留落胥餘，仁頻并閭，欃檀木蘭，豫章女貞，長千仞，大連抱，夸條直暢，實葉葰茂，攢立叢倚，連卷累佹，崔錯委骫，阬衡閜砢，垂條扶於，落英幡纚，紛容蕭蔘，旖旎從風，瀏蒞芔吸，蓋象金石之聲，管籥之音。

translations.literal: Sand pear and oak, chinquapin, birch and sumac, oleaster and catalpa, betel and fir, sandalwood and magnolia, camphor and privet — a thousand ren tall, so thick that several arms cannot embrace them, their branches spreading straight and far, their fruit and leaves lush and dense. They stand in clustered ranks, intertwined and layered, towering and tangled, filling hollows and crossing ridges. Their hanging branches sway and droop, fallen petals flutter in streamers. They rustle and sway, bending gracefully with the wind, their sounds echoing like the notes of bronze and stone, the tones of pipes and flutes.
translations.idiomatic: Sand pear and oak, chinquapin, birch and sumac, oleaster and catalpa, betel and fir, sandalwood and magnolia, camphor and privet — a thousand ren tall, so vast that several arms cannot encircle them, their branches spreading straight and far, their fruit and leaves lush and dense. They stand in clustered ranks, intertwined and layered, towering and tangled, filling hollows and crossing ridges. Their hanging branches sway and droop; fallen petals flutter in streamers. They rustle and bend gracefully with the wind, their sounds like the notes of bronze and stone, the tones of pipes and flutes.

sentences.zh: 柴池茈虒，旋環后宮，雜遝累輯，被山緣谷，循阪下隰，視之無端，究之無窮。

translations.literal: Firewood pools and purple rushes circle the rear palace; jumbled and layered, they cover the mountains and line the valleys, follow the slopes and descend to the wetlands — look, and there is no beginning; investigate, and there is no end.
translations.idiomatic: Brushwood pools and purple rushes encircle the rear palace. Jumbled and layered, they cover the mountains and line the valleys, follow the slopes and descend to the wetlands — look and there is no beginning; seek and there is no end.

sentences.zh: 「於是玄猨素雌，蜼玃飛鸓，蛭蜩蠷蝚，螹胡豰蛫，棲息乎其閒；

translations.literal: Then there are black apes and white females, long-tailed monkeys and gibbons, flying squirrels, leeches and cicadas, crested gibbons, spotted weasels and crabs — they rest and dwell among them;
translations.idiomatic: There dwell among them black apes and their white mates, long-tailed monkeys and gibbons, flying squirrels, cicadas, crested gibbons, spotted weasels, and crabs;

sentences.zh: 長嘯哀鳴，翩幡互經，夭蟜枝格，偃蹇杪顛。

translations.literal: They howl long and wail mournfully, flutter and wheel in crisscrossing paths, swinging lithely among the branches, sprawling at the tips and tops of the trees.
translations.idiomatic: they howl and wail, flutter and wheel in crisscrossing paths, swing lithely among the branches, and sprawl at the tips and tops of the trees.

sentences.zh: 於是乎隃絕梁，騰殊榛，捷垂條，踔稀閒，牢落陸離，爛曼遠遷。

translations.literal: Then they leap across broken ridges, spring over dense thickets, snatch at hanging branches, vault across open spaces, scattered and dazzling, tumbling far and wide.
translations.idiomatic: They leap across broken ridges, spring over dense thickets, catch hold of hanging branches, and vault across open spaces — scattered and dazzling, tumbling far and wide.

sentences.zh: 「若此輩者，數千百處。

translations.literal: Scenes such as these number in the thousands and hundreds.
translations.idiomatic: Scenes such as these can be found in thousands of places.

sentences.zh: 嬉游往來，宮宿館舍，庖廚不徙，後宮不移，百官備具。

translations.literal: For traveling and pleasure excursions, there are palaces and lodges at every stop, with kitchens that need not be relocated, the rear palace that need not be moved, and a full complement of officials.
translations.idiomatic: For excursions and pleasure trips, there are palaces and lodges at every stop, with kitchens that need not be relocated, a rear palace that need not be moved, and a full complement of officials always in attendance.

sentences.zh: 「於是乎背秋涉冬，天子校獵。

translations.literal: Then, as autumn turns to winter, the Son of Heaven goes on an imperial hunt.
translations.idiomatic: Then, as autumn turns into winter, the Son of Heaven goes forth on an imperial hunt.

sentences.zh: 乘鏤象，六玉虯，拖蜺旌，靡雲旗，前皮軒，后道游；

translations.literal: He rides in a carved ivory chariot, drawn by six jade-colored dragons, trailing rainbow pennants and cloud banners, with leather-canopied carriages in front and pleasure chariots behind;
translations.idiomatic: He rides in a chariot carved with ivory, drawn by six jade-colored dragons. Rainbow pennants trail behind and cloud banners flutter; leather-canopied carriages go before him and pleasure chariots follow behind;

sentences.zh: 孫叔奉轡，衛公驂乘，扈從橫行，出乎四校之中。

translations.literal: Lord Sun holds the reins, Lord Wei rides as outrider; the retinue charges in formation, issuing from the midst of the four battalions.
translations.idiomatic: Lord Sun holds the reins and Lord Wei rides as outrider. The retinue charges in formation, issuing forth from the midst of the four battalions.

sentences.zh: 鼓嚴簿，縱獠者，江河為阹，泰山為櫓，車騎雷起，隱天動地，先後陸離，離散別追，淫淫裔裔，緣陵流澤，雲布雨施。」

translations.literal: Drums beat the solemn command, the hunters are loosed. The rivers serve as barriers, Mount Tai as a lookout tower. Chariots and cavalry rise like thunder, shaking heaven and earth. The vanguard and rear dazzle in splendid array; they scatter and give chase in separate parties, streaming and flowing, following the ridges and coursing through the marshes, spreading like clouds and falling like rain."
translations.idiomatic: The drums beat the solemn command and the hunters are loosed. Rivers serve as barriers and Mount Tai itself as a watchtower. Chariots and cavalry thunder forth, shaking heaven and earth. The vanguard and rear blaze in splendid array; they scatter and give separate chase, streaming and flowing, following the ridges and coursing through the marshes, spreading like clouds and falling like rain."

sentences.zh: 「生貔豹，搏豺狼，手熊羆，足野羊，蒙鹖蘇，绔白虎，被豳文，跨野馬。

translations.literal: They seize leopards alive, wrestle jackals and wolves, grapple bears bare-handed, trample wild sheep underfoot. Wearing pheasant-feather cloaks, riding white tigers, draped in the patterned garb of Bin, they stride upon wild horses.
translations.idiomatic: They seize leopards alive and wrestle jackals and wolves, grapple bears with their bare hands and trample wild sheep underfoot. Clad in pheasant-feather cloaks, they ride upon white tigers, draped in the patterned garb of Bin, striding upon wild horses.

sentences.zh: 陵三嵏之危，下磧歷之坻；

translations.literal: They climb the perilous heights of the three peaks, descend the rocky gravel ridges;
translations.idiomatic: They scale the perilous heights of the three peaks and descend the rocky gravel ridges;

sentences.zh: 俓陖赴險，越壑厲水。

translations.literal: They dash straight up steep slopes to meet danger, leap over ravines and ford streams.
translations.idiomatic: they dash straight up steep slopes into danger, leap over ravines, and ford rushing streams.

sentences.zh: 推蜚廉，弄解豸，格瑕蛤，鋋猛氏，罥騕褭，射封豕。

translations.literal: They chase down the feilian beast, toy with the xiezhi, grapple with giant clams, spear the fierce ones, lasso the yaoniao, and shoot the great boar.
translations.idiomatic: They chase down the feilian beast, toy with the xiezhi, grapple with giant clams, spear the fierce creatures, lasso the yaoniao, and shoot the great boar.

sentences.zh: 箭不茍害，解脰陷腦；

translations.literal: No arrow harms carelessly — each splits the neck and pierces the skull;
translations.idiomatic: No arrow is wasted — each splits a neck and pierces a skull;

sentences.zh: 弓不虛發，應聲而倒。

translations.literal: No bow is drawn in vain — the beasts fall at the sound of the bowstring.
translations.idiomatic: no bow is drawn in vain — each beast falls the instant the bowstring sounds.

sentences.zh: 於是乎乘輿彌節裴回，翺翔往來，睨部曲之進退，覽將率之變態。

translations.literal: Thereupon the imperial carriage slows and lingers, gliding back and forth, the emperor casting sidelong glances at the advancing and retreating squadrons, surveying the ever-changing tactics of the commanders.
translations.idiomatic: Then the imperial carriage slows and lingers, gliding back and forth. The emperor casts sidelong glances at the advancing and retreating squadrons, surveying the ever-changing tactics of the commanders.

sentences.zh: 然後浸潭促節，儵夐遠去，流離輕禽，蹴履狡獸，轊白鹿，捷狡兔，軼赤電，遺光燿，追怪物，出宇宙，彎繁弱，滿白羽，射游梟，櫟蜚虡，擇肉后發，先中命處，弦矢分，藝殪仆。

translations.literal: Then he quickens the pace, darts far in an instant, scattering nimble birds, trampling cunning beasts, running down white deer, catching crafty hares, outstripping red lightning, leaving radiance behind, pursuing strange beasts beyond the bounds of the world. He bends the mighty bow Fanruo, fills it with white-feathered arrows, shoots the flying owl, pierces the hovering pheasant, choosing his mark before he lets fly, striking the vital spot before the arrow leaves the string — the bowstring twangs, and the beast falls dead.
translations.idiomatic: Then he quickens the pace and darts far in an instant, scattering nimble birds and trampling cunning beasts, running down white deer and catching crafty hares, outstripping red lightning and leaving radiance behind, pursuing strange beasts beyond the bounds of the world. He bends the mighty Fanruo bow and fills it with white-feathered arrows, shooting the flying owl and piercing the hovering pheasant. He chooses his mark before he lets fly, striking the vital spot even as the arrow leaves the string — the bowstring twangs and the beast falls dead.

sentences.zh: 「然後揚節而上浮，陵驚風，歷駭飚，乘虛無，與神俱，轔玄鶴，亂昆雞。

translations.literal: Then, raising the pace, he soars upward, surmounting the startling winds, passing through fearsome gales, riding the void, traveling with the spirits, overtaking black cranes, and scattering the Kunlun pheasants.
translations.idiomatic: Then, urging the pace higher, he soars upward, surmounting the startling winds, passing through fearsome gales, riding the void itself, traveling alongside the spirits. He overtakes black cranes and scatters the Kunlun pheasants.

sentences.zh: 遒孔鸞，促鵔鸃，拂鹥鳥，捎鳳皇，捷鴛雛，掩焦明。

translations.literal: He pursues peacocks and phoenixes, presses the junyi bird, brushes the yi bird, clips the fenghuang, catches mandarin ducks, and captures the jiaoming.
translations.idiomatic: He pursues peacocks and phoenixes, presses the junyi bird, brushes past the yi bird, clips the fenghuang, catches mandarin ducklings, and captures the jiaoming.

sentences.zh: 「道盡涂殫，迴車而還。

translations.literal: When every road has been exhausted and every path run out, the chariot is turned and they head home.
translations.idiomatic: When every road has been exhausted and every path run out, the chariot turns for home.

sentences.zh: 招搖乎襄羊，降集乎北纮，率乎直指，闇乎反鄉。

translations.literal: Waving and beckoning in leisurely procession, they descend and gather at the northern bounds, advancing straight ahead, turning back toward home in the dimness.
translations.idiomatic: They wave in leisurely procession, descend and gather at the northern bounds, advance straight ahead, and turn homeward as darkness falls.

sentences.zh: 蹶（闕）［關］，歷封巒，過鳷鵲，望露寒，下棠梨，息宜春，西馳宣曲，濯鹢牛首，登龍臺，掩細柳，觀士大夫之勤略，鈞獠者之所得獲。

translations.literal: They pass the gates, cross the sealed mountains, pass the Zhique terrace, gaze at the Cold Dew pavilion, descend at Tangli, rest at Yichun, race westward to Xuanqu, wash at the Cormorant Pool of Niushou, ascend the Dragon Terrace, sweep past Xiliu, surveying the diligence and strategy of the officers and gentlemen, and tallying what the hunters have caught.
translations.idiomatic: They pass the gates, cross the sealed ridges, pass the Zhique terrace, gaze at the Cold Dew pavilion, descend at Tangli and rest at Yichun. They race westward to Xuanqu, bathe in the Cormorant Pool of Niushou, ascend the Dragon Terrace, and sweep past Xiliu — surveying the diligence and tactics of the officers, tallying everything the hunters have caught.

sentences.zh: 徒車之所轔轢，乘騎之所蹂若，人民之所蹈躤，與其窮極倦劇，驚憚慴伏，不被創刃而死者，佗佗籍籍，填阬滿谷，揜平彌澤。

translations.literal: What the foot soldiers' chariots have run over, what the mounted riders have trampled, what the common people have trodden upon, together with those that were exhausted, terrified and cowering, or died from fright without being touched by blade or wound — they lie heaped and scattered, filling pits and valleys, covering the level ground and flooding the marshes.
translations.idiomatic: What the foot soldiers' chariots have run over, what the mounted riders have trampled, what the common men have trodden — together with beasts that were driven to exhaustion, terrified into submission, or died of fright without being touched by a single blade — they lie heaped and scattered, filling pits and valleys, blanketing the level ground and flooding the marshes.

sentences.zh: 「於是乎游戲懈怠，置酒乎昊天之臺，張樂乎轇輵之宇；

translations.literal: Then, when all is done and rest begins, wine is laid out at the Terrace of Vast Heaven, and music is performed in the Hall of the Revolving Heavens;
translations.idiomatic: Then, with the sport concluded and rest begun, wine is laid out at the Terrace of Vast Heaven, and music fills the Hall of the Revolving Heavens;

sentences.zh: 撞千石之鐘，立萬石之鉅；

translations.literal: They strike bells weighing a thousand stones and erect stands weighing ten thousand stones;
translations.idiomatic: bells weighing a thousand stones are struck, and racks weighing ten thousand stones are raised;

sentences.zh: 建翠華之旗，樹靈鼉之鼓。

translations.literal: They raise banners of kingfisher plumes and set up drums of sacred crocodile hide.
translations.idiomatic: banners of kingfisher plumes are raised, and drums of sacred crocodile hide are set up.

sentences.zh: 奏陶唐氏之舞，聽葛天氏之歌，千人唱，萬人和，山陵為之震動，川谷為之蕩波。

translations.literal: They perform the dances of Emperor Yao's era and listen to the songs of Ge Tian's time. A thousand voices chant, ten thousand voices harmonize; the mountains and hills tremble, and the rivers and valleys surge with waves.
translations.idiomatic: They perform the dances of Emperor Yao's age and listen to the songs of Ge Tian's time. A thousand voices chant and ten thousand harmonize — the mountains and hills tremble, and the rivers and valleys surge with waves.

sentences.zh: 巴俞宋蔡，淮南于遮，文成顛歌，族舉遞奏，金鼓迭起，鏗鎗鐺鼞，洞心駭耳。

translations.literal: Dances of Ba and Yu, songs of Song and Cai, entertainments of Huainan and Yuzhe, the Wencheng and Dian songs — groups perform in alternation, bronze bells and drums rising in succession, clanging and booming, piercing the heart and startling the ears.
translations.idiomatic: The dances of Ba and Yu, the songs of Song and Cai, the entertainments of Huainan and Yuzhe, the Wencheng and Dian songs — troupes perform in alternation, bronze bells and drums rising in succession, clanging and booming, piercing the heart and startling the ears.

sentences.zh: 荊吳鄭衛之聲，韶濩武象之樂，陰淫案衍之音，鄢郢繽紛，激楚結風，俳優侏儒，狄鞮之倡，所以娛耳目而樂心意者，麗靡爛漫於前，靡曼美色於後。

translations.literal: The sounds of Jing and Wu, Zheng and Wei, the music of the Shao, the Hu, the Wu, and the Xiang, the lingering and languid melodies, the dazzling profusion of Yan and Ying, the stirring Chu airs and binding wind songs, jesters and dwarfs, the Di-Di performers — all that could delight the eyes and ears and gladden the heart and mind — gorgeous and dazzling before them, lovely beauties behind.
translations.idiomatic: The sounds of Jing and Wu, Zheng and Wei, the music of the Shao, the Hu, the Wu, and the Xiang, lingering and languid melodies, the dazzling profusion of Yan and Ying, stirring Chu airs and binding wind songs, jesters and dwarfs, performers from the Di-Di — everything to delight the eyes and ears and gladden the heart — gorgeous and dazzling before, and lovely beauties behind.

sentences.zh: 「若夫青琴宓妃之徒，絕殊離俗，姣冶嫻都，靚莊刻飭，便嬛綽約，柔橈嬛嬛，娬媚姌嫋；

translations.literal: As for those like Qingqin and the Lady of the Luo River, who are of surpassing beauty and far removed from the common world — alluring and refined, radiantly adorned and meticulously groomed, graceful and lissome, supple and sinuous, enchanting and willowy;
translations.idiomatic: As for those like Qingqin and the Lady of the Luo, who are of surpassing beauty far removed from the common world — alluring and refined, radiantly adorned and meticulously groomed, graceful and lissome, supple and sinuous, enchanting and willowy;

sentences.zh: 抴獨繭之褕袘，眇閻易以戌削，媥姺徶屑，與世殊服；

translations.literal: They trail robes of single-silk weave with flowing trains, slender and elegant with cinched waists, swaying and swirling, their garments unlike any in the world;
translations.idiomatic: they trail robes of single-silk weave with flowing trains, slender and elegant with cinched waists, swaying and swirling — their garments unlike any in the world;

sentences.zh: 芬香漚郁，酷烈淑郁；

translations.literal: Their fragrance is rich and enveloping, intense and sweetly fragrant;
translations.idiomatic: their fragrance is rich and enveloping, intensely sweet;

sentences.zh: 皓齒粲爛，宜笑旳皪；

translations.literal: Their white teeth gleam brilliantly, their smiles are radiant and sparkling;
translations.idiomatic: their white teeth gleam brilliantly, their smiles radiant and sparkling;

sentences.zh: 長眉連娟，微睇綿藐；

translations.literal: Their long eyebrows curve gracefully, their gentle glances are soft and distant;
translations.idiomatic: their long eyebrows curve gracefully, and their gentle sidelong glances are soft and distant;

sentences.zh: 色授魂與，心愉於側。

translations.literal: With their charms they bestow their souls, and one's heart is gladdened at their side.
translations.idiomatic: with their beauty they captivate the very soul, and the heart is gladdened in their presence.

sentences.zh: 「於是酒中樂酣，天子芒然而思，似若有亡。

translations.literal: Then, amid the wine and at the height of the music, the Son of Heaven grows pensive and lost in thought, as if something were missing.
translations.idiomatic: Then, amid the wine and at the height of the revelry, the Son of Heaven grows pensive, as though something were missing.

sentences.zh: 曰：『嗟乎，此泰奢侈！

translations.literal: He says: 'Alas, this is far too extravagant!
translations.idiomatic: He says, 'Alas, this is far too extravagant!

sentences.zh: 朕以覽聽餘閒，無事棄日，順天道以殺伐，時休息於此，恐後世靡麗，遂往而不反，非所以為繼嗣創業垂統也。』

translations.literal: I have used my leisure time for sightseeing, wasting idle days, following the Way of Heaven in seasonal hunts, and resting here from time to time. Yet I fear that future generations will be seduced by such splendor, plunging onward without return — this is no way to establish a legacy and hand down a dynasty.'
translations.idiomatic: I have used my leisure for sightseeing, passing idle days, following the Way of Heaven in seasonal hunts, and resting here from time to time. But I fear that future generations will be seduced by such splendor and plunge onward without return — this is no way to found a legacy or hand down a dynasty.'

sentences.zh: 於是乃解酒罷獵，而命有司曰：『地可以墾辟，悉為農郊，以贍萌隸；

translations.literal: Thereupon he ends the drinking and calls off the hunt, and commands the officials: 'All land that can be cleared and cultivated shall be made into farmland on the outskirts, to provide for the common people;
translations.idiomatic: Thereupon he ends the drinking and calls off the hunt, commanding the officials, 'All land fit for cultivation shall be turned over to farmland for the common people;

sentences.zh: 隤墻填塹，使山澤之民得至焉。

translations.literal: Tear down the walls and fill in the moats, so that the people of the mountains and marshes may enter freely.
